metadata.title: Why responding to a webhook after processing it silently doubles every event
metadata.topic: Webhook response ordering and the retry loop it controls
metadata.purpose: Webhook platforms measure round-trip latency from the receiver's side; a late 200 is indistinguishable from a dropped packet, so the platform retries. Responding first, then processing async, terminates the retry loop before it fires.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: request/response model, webhook delivery semantics, idempotency
metadata.exclusions: HMAC URL validation, queue-based retry systems
metadata.visual_object: Two vertical timelines (server ↔ handler) — left: process-first → 3s timeout → retry → second session; right: 200-first → async processing → no retry → one session.
metadata.hook: Your session logger records every meeting start — but some sessions appear twice, only in production, only under load.

beats.narration_text: A handler receives `meeting.rtms_started`, writes to a database, then sends 200. The write takes 4s. The platform waited 3s, declared the endpoint unresponsive, and re-delivered. Two sessions open; two rows inserted.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: HTTP 200 and the database write are logically independent — why does their ordering decide whether the event fires once or twice?
Your session logger records every meeting start — but some sessions appear twice, only in production, only under load.

Webhook platforms measure round-trip latency from the receiver's side; a late 200 is indistinguishable from a dropped packet, so the platform retries. Responding first, then processing async, terminates the retry loop before it fires.
beats.visual: Two vertical timelines (server ↔ handler) — left: process-first → 3s timeout → retry → second session; right: 200-first → async processing → no retry → one session.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Event at t=0, DB write 4s, timeout 3s → retry at t=3, 200 at t=4 → 2 sessions, 2 rows. Move `res.status(200).send()` to line 1 → 200 at t=0.001, write done t=4, no retry → one session.

Webhook platforms measure round-trip latency from the receiver's side; a late 200 is indistinguishable from a dropped packet, so the platform retries. Responding first, then processing async, terminates the retry loop before it fires.
